channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Intel évoque la puce qui propulsera peut-être votre prochaine console portable
item.description: Évoquées au CES et un peu perdues de vue depuis, les puces Panther Lake destinées aux consoles de jeu portables refont parler d'elles. Leurs spécifications se précisent et l'on sait désormais, de source sûre, quel sera leur nom.

item.title: ‘You feel radicalized’: A Meta AI exec watched agents beat her top workers. Now she’s built a nonprofit to help Gen Z find jobs before they disappear
item.description: Entry level jobs are being done by AI, and that has left Gen Z up a river without a paddle

item.title: Would you buy a ChatGPT-powered iPhone rival? OpenAI is reportedly developing a smartphone chip, which teases the possibility of a full-blown OpenAI mobile
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly working on a smartphone chipset — one that would put AI at its heart and reduce our reliance on apps.

item.title: Xiaomi s’installe chez BMW : son plan audacieux pour conquérir l’Europe avec des ingénieurs Porsche et Lamborghini
item.description: Pour concurrencer les Allemands, Xiaomi va droit au but en ouvrant un centre R&D dans le bastion historique de BMW, Munich. Mieux encore, la marque chinoise repêche des anciens de BMW, Porsche et Lamborghini pour ses futurs modèles.

item.title: DJI Mic Mini 2 review: The tiny wireless mic is colorful and much cheaper
item.description: Not long ago, high-quality wireless mic systems cost over $500 and required a bulky transmitter and lav mic wire tucked under the subject’s shirt. DJI’s Mic Mini showed how unnecessary all of that was, combining mic and transmitter into a 0.35-ounce device that delivered high quality sound for under $100. Now, DJI has released the Mic Mini 2 (around $100) as a mild update to the original at a much lower price. It’s more fashion forward thanks to the colorful new magnetic covers and offers an audio upgrade via new voice tone presets. Those changes make it the best budget wireless mic for new bu...

item.title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools
item.description: Otter is also releasing a new Windows app that can capture meeting notes without joining one

item.title: I tested the DJI Mic Mini 2 and the thing that excited me the most is the new low pricing — there’s no better value small wireless mic
item.description: The differences between this second-gen edition of DJI's Mic Mini and the original are mostly cosmetic, but at this price it seems unfair to quibble.

item.title: 'Please be fake' — PlayStation fans panic as Sony seemingly introduces ridiculous new '30-day license check' on digital games
item.description: Sony is under fire yet again in 2026, but it's not due to increasing PlayStation hardware prices, but rather a new rumored DRM measure.

item.title: ‘The future has arrived’: China’s power grid will soon be run by an army of humanoid robots and robot dogs as state announces $1 billion investment in 8,500 robo-helpers
item.description: The Chinese government spent $1 billion on AI-driven robots as a new report says the US’s power grid is struggling.

item.title: Taylor Swift is taking legal action against the rising threat of AI misuse — here’s why the singer has filed trademark applications to protect her identity
item.description: Taylor Swift has filed trademark applications to protect her sound and image, and I think other global artists will follow.

item.title: InkPoster Tela 28.5 Review: A Luxe Home Digital Frame
item.description: It’s the world’s best E Ink picture frame, but you’ll need Monet money to make the most of it.

item.title: Mistral AI launches Workflows, a Temporal-powered orchestration engine already running millions of daily executions
item.description: Mistral AI, the Paris-based artificial intelligence company valued at €11.7 billion ($13.8 billion), today released Workflows in public preview — a production-grade orchestration layer designed to move enterprise AI systems out of proofs of concept and into the business processes that generate revenue.The product, which launches as part of Mistral's Studio platform, is the company's clearest articulation yet of a thesis that is quietly reshaping the enterprise AI market: that the bottleneck for organizations adopting AI is no longer the model itself, but the infrastructure required to run it r...

item.title: ‘This will not end well’: Apple App Store gets new ‘more affordable’ monthly subscriptions, but not all iPhone owners are convinced
item.description: App Store users can now take out annual app subscriptions and pay for them on a monthly basis.

item.title: Microsoft se débarrasse d’un élément insignifiant lié à Windows 8
item.description: D’une version à l’autre, les systèmes d’exploitation héritent d’éléments graphiques issus de leurs prédécesseurs. Des éléments qui peuvent être oubliés à l’image de cette icône de chargement présente sur Windows 11, vieille de 14 ans.

item.title: 'When you look at horror franchises or movies or games, the sci-fi space is huge' — Directive 8020 executive producer says the simple reason for the game's space setting was because it's 'just fun'
item.description: According to Directive 8020 executive producer, Dan McDonald, Supermassive always knew that it would make a Dark Pictures game set in space.

item.title: OpenAI and Microsoft make a huge change to their exclusive partnership - does this open the door for AWS to swoop in?
item.description: OpenAI says it wants to serve all customers regardless of cloud provider, but Microsoft remains a "primary" provider.

item.title: Windows 11 : cette nouvelle option va sauver vos yeux (et soulager vos migraines)
item.description: Windows 11 devrait accueillir une nouvelle fonctionnalité de « teinte d'écran », pour reposer vos yeux en réduisant la quantité de lumière bleue grâce à une teinte de couleur spécifique.

item.title: WIRED’s Smart Home Ecosystem Guide (2026)
item.description: If you’re waffling between Alexa, Siri, and Google, the answer may already be in your home.

item.title: My secret to remembering people
item.description: Or, why you should have a ‘People’ note. Let me tell you my trick for remembering the names of people I meet: I don’t.

item.title: Score T-Mobiles 5G home internet for free with up to $200 cash back — how to claim now
item.description: T-Mobile is offering a month free of its 5G home internet on all three of its plans and up to $200 cash back when you sign up.

item.title: Google and Pentagon reportedly agree on deal for ‘any lawful’ use of AI
item.description: Google has signed a classified deal that allows the US Department of Defense to use its AI models for "any lawful government purpose," The Information reports. The agreement was reported less than a day after Google employees demanded CEO Sundar Pichai block the Pentagon from using its AI amid concerns that it would be used […]

item.title: Mistral AI piégé par la propagande russe : le chatbot français répète les fake news dans plus d’un cas sur deux
item.description: Mistral AI, la pépite française de l'intelligence artificielle, a un talon d'Achille. Une récente étude de NewsGuard révèle que son chatbot grand public relaie des campagnes de propagande d'État dans plus de la moitié des cas.

item.title: Hulu is quietly developing a brutal TV show pilot that could rival Yellowstone — and it's even 'copied' a famous Taylor Sheridan casting tradition
item.description: Hulu has ordered a pilot of graphic novel Southern Bastards — and if it sticks, the TV show could rival Yellowstone in more ways than one.

item.title: Geopolitical tensions create new risks for satellite operators globally
item.description: India banned a satellite operator because of who owns it - could the Iran war encourage others to follow suit worldwide?

item.title: New screen tint feature discovered in Windows 11 is designed to help with eye strain and even combating migraines
item.description: Windows 11 is getting a screen tint feature to help guard against eye strain, and migraines, and it could even help with photophobia.

item.title: Attack of the killer script kiddies
item.description: Last August, some of the best cybersecurity teams in the business gathered in Las Vegas to demonstrate the strength of their AI bug-finding systems at DARPA's Artificial Intelligence Cyber Challenge (AIxCC). The tools had scanned 54 million lines of actual software code that DARPA had injected with artificial flaws. The teams were capable enough to […]...

item.title: The 4 Best Laptop Power Banks We've Tested (2026)
item.description: If you’re looking for the perfect laptop power bank, I’ve tested ‘em all, and these are the best.

item.title: The blueprint architecture for securing the AI data center
item.description: AI data center security cannot be an afterthought.

item.title: Magic: The Gathering Arena developers intend to form a union with the CWA
item.description: Magic: The Gathering Arena developers at Hasbro subsidiary Wizards of the Coast are set to join the Communications Workers of America (CWA), the union announced. The CWA says it has secured a "supermajority" among workers in favor of unionization for the chapter, called United Wizards of the Coast (UWOTC-CWA). The CWA has filed for a formal election with the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB), but that will be withdrawn if Hasbro voluntarily recognizes the union by May 1st. "At Wizards, we’re organizing for a say in layoffs, accountability that runs up and down the chain, and a living wage ...

item.title: Samsung's Galaxy phones could be getting a big camera redesign to facilitate MagSafe-style charging — here are 4 ways they could look different
item.description: Samsung might change the placement and design of the rear cameras on future phones, both to freshen things up and to allow for built-in magnets.

item.title: I compared ChatGPT Images 2.0 and Google’s Nano Banana 2 using real-world prompts — from portraits to product shots — and the AI image generator that came out on top genuinely surprised me
item.description: ChatGPT Images 2.0 and Nano Banana 2 both impressed, but ChatGPT stood out for creating edits that feel more like real photos rather than polished enhancements.

item.title: Sosh lance son offre « Boost Fibre » avec 8 Gbit/s et Wi-Fi 7, mais fait trois sacrifices
item.description: Sosh lance une nouvelle offre « Sosh Boost Fibre » avec 8 Gbit/s de débit descendant et le Wi-Fi 7. Mais il va falloir faire quelques compromis.

item.title: L’Europe révèle comment Google devra ouvrir Android à ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity et les autres
item.description: L'Europe veut que votre assistant vocal ne soit plus choisi par Google ou Apple, mais par vous. Les deux géants n'ont pas du tout la même réponse.

item.title: Stephen King jumps to Jimmy Kimmels defence after Melania Trump calls for him to be fired
item.description: Horror author Stephen King has defended Jimmy Kimmel after Melania Trump called for him to be fired on X.

item.title: Decathlon enlève 700 € à son Rockrider E-Feel 900 S, un puissant VTTAE tout suspendu
item.description: Le Rockrider E-Feel 900 S, bien plus abordable que la version Team Edition, reste un VTT électrique puissant, polyvalent et rassurant. Actuellement, Decathlon le propose à 3 798,99 euros au lieu de 4 499,99 euros.

item.title: The 4 best vacuum cleaners for seasonal allergies, recommended by a home tech expert — beat hayfever with powerful suction, HEPA filters, and sealed dust bags
item.description: Don't get caught out by rising pollen counts — these top-rated vacuums use powerful suction, HEPA filters, and sealed dust bags to keep allergens at bay.

item.title: Quantum can wait: Why CISOs should focus on today’s preventable cyber risks
item.description: The criminals of today are still exploiting basic security failures.

item.title: Stop Trying to Unmask Satoshi Nakamoto
item.description: On this week’s Big Interview podcast, actor-director Ben McKenzie talks about the rise of crypto, why he finds it dangerous, and why it benefits from having a mysterious creator.

item.title: ‘We are very nerdy’: An exclusive interview with Ikea’s top designer
item.description: Ikea’s global design manager Johan Ejdemo chats about the company’s priorities in 2026, the changing shape of its products, and the role AI is and isn’t playing. Ikea design manager Johan Ejdemo is looking years into the future.

item.title: Addicted to social media? This adorable, infuriating cat will stop you from doomscrolling
item.description: A new Chrome extension lets a lazy cat block your browser when you’ve spent too much time on social media. There are plenty of different hacks, tools, and apps designed to break social media addiction or otherwise reduce your screen time. This is just the cutest one.

item.title: How to watch A Taste for Murder online for FREE – stream sun-soaked crime drama from anywhere
item.description: When a grieving British detective heads to Italy for a sabbatical, police work isn't the plan, but where Joe Mottram goes, murder follows. Here's how to watch A Taste for Murder online from anywhere.
